name: Swift Title Loans
description_short: Swift Title Loans at 4246 S New Braunfels Ave #115, San Antonio, TX 78223 in the Highlands neighborhood is Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Call +1 210-791-9531.
description_long:
Swift Title Loans operates a car title lending business at 1473 S Trezevant St in Memphis, Tennessee. The company specializes in providing fast cash loans secured by vehicle titles, positioning itself as an accessible option for consumers with poor credit histories, past bankruptcies, or limited traditional borrowing options. Founded on a model that accepts applicants regardless of credit score, the company leverages the vehicle itself as collateral rather than creditworthiness.

The company offers car title loans ranging from $100 to $50,000, with a primary marketing hook of "sign up by noon, get cash today." Applicants can complete online applications, and the loan structure allows borrowers to retain use of their vehicle while the title is held as collateral. The business operates Monday–Friday 9am–7pm and Saturday 10am–5pm, and can be reached at 1-888-903-0217 or service@swiftloans.com. The website emphasizes 256-bit SSL and TLS security for online transactions.

What distinguishes Swift Title Loans is its explicit welcome of borrowers with damaged credit profiles, particularly those with past bankruptcies. Customer reviews consistently mention a staff member named Alfred who is credited with simplifying the application process and patiently explaining terms. The company positions itself as transparent and trustworthy, with customers noting the straightforward nature of the process relative to their expectations.

However, the company operates in a high-risk lending category with inherent structural risks. While reviews are positive regarding service quality, there is no disclosed information about APR, fees, or repayment terms on the website—critical data points for assessing actual cost. The reviews, though numerous, focus heavily on service experience rather than loan value or outcomes, and all are attributed to the same staff member (Alfred), raising questions about review authenticity and representativeness. Title loans carry the inherent risk of vehicle repossession for non-payment, a material risk not explicitly discussed on the site.
address: 4246 S New Braunfels Ave #115, San Antonio, TX 78223

company_info.headquarters: 4246 S New Braunfels Ave #115, San Antonio, TX 78223

pros: Same-day funding available for applicants who sign up by noon
Accepts borrowers with bad credit and past bankruptcies without rejection
Wide loan range ($100–$50,000) accommodates various financial needs
Borrowers can keep and use their vehicle during the loan term
256-bit SSL and TLS encryption for online security
Staff assistance available during application and paperwork process
Extended hours (Mon–Fri until 7pm, Saturday 10am–5pm) for working customers
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee structure disclosed on website—cost of borrowing is opaque
Title loans carry repossession risk if payments are missed; this material risk is not prominently disclosed
Customer reviews appear concentrated around one staff member (Alfred) and lack diversity in reviewer experiences or outcomes
No information provided about payment plan options, despite customer requests for more flexibility
Limited digital tools: no mention of mobile app, online payment portal, or automated reminders despite customer suggestions
best_for: Borrowers with poor credit or bankruptcy history who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans
Consumers needing emergency cash ($500–$2,000) within 24 hours and willing to use vehicle as collateral
Individuals who own a paid-off or nearly paid-off vehicle and need short-term liquidity
diagnosis: Swift Title Loans is best suited for borrowers with poor credit who need emergency cash within 24 hours and own a vehicle outright or nearly outright. The primary caveat is that APR and fee information are completely absent from the website, making it impossible to assess true cost; additionally, the risk of vehicle repossession for non-payment is inherent to title loans but not explicitly highlighted, and borrowers should understand they are using their vehicle as collateral for short-term cash.
services: Same-day funding for applicants approved by noon
Loan service for borrowers with bad credit or past bankruptcies
Vehicle retention during loan term (borrower keeps car)
